methods.name: Peer-Median
methods.rationale: Median across 3 same-sector + same-stage peers in Chips & Semiconductors. Range: $1.0B - $23.0B.

methods.name: Public-Comp Anchoring
methods.skip_reason: target revenue not on file (private companies rarely disclose)
methods.rationale: Public Semiconductors P/S median is 20.3x across 3 tickers. Cannot apply without target's revenue.

private.name: Cerebras
private.rationale: Same sector (Chips & Semiconductors), same stage (Public), post-money $23B

private.name: Ouster
private.rationale: Same sector (Chips & Semiconductors), same stage (Public), post-money $1.5B

private.name: Aeva Technologies
private.rationale: Same sector (Chips & Semiconductors), same stage (Public), post-money $1B+

data.narrative:
Credo Semiconductor is a public-stage company in Chips & Semiconductors that went public via IPO in 2022. The valuation analysis draws from a peer universe of 3 same-sector, same-stage public comparables: Cerebras ($23.0B), Ouster ($1.5B), and Aeva Technologies ($1.0B), alongside 8 private frontier-tech peers in the semiconductor space.

The peer-median method is the applicable triangulation approach, given that Credo's revenue is not disclosed. The 3 public semiconductor comparables yield a median valuation of $1.5B, with a range spanning $1.0B to $23.0B. Public-comp anchoring via price-to-sales multiples—the sector median stands at 20.3x across ALAB, NVDA, and AMD—cannot be applied without target revenue data. This constraint is typical for private and recently public frontier-tech firms.

The analysis supports a valuation range of $1.3B to $1.7B, centered on $1.5B. This reflects the median of the peer set. Key uncertainties include the wide dispersion in the comp universe (Cerebras trades at 23x the floor valuation), which signals high volatility in frontier semiconductor valuations and limited precision around any single point estimate. Additionally, the absence of revenue limits the deployment of revenue-based multiples, a standard secondary validation tool in semiconductor valuations.
